observations: A hairless gray humanoid crouches low on long arms and bent legs while numerous black blade-edged tendrils radiate from its back in sweeping arcs.
The smooth oval head has two deep black eye cavities and no visible mouth or nose; seam-like lines, scratches, and geometric marks continue across the head and body.
Long fingers and toes end in dark claws, and the planted four-limb stance places the creature close to the ground as if stalking or preparing to spring.
The pale compact body is surrounded by a much larger dark circular field of tendrils, making separation from the appendages the central visual tension.
defects: The many similarly curved tendrils overlap and cross behind the body, making their count, attachment points, and individual motion difficult to follow.
The tendril halo occupies more visual area than the humanoid and can dominate the unit's identity over the body itself.
The uniformly gray body surface provides limited material separation between skin, mask-like head, muscle, and scar or seam structures.
uncertainties: The image does not resolve whether the oval head is a biological face, a fused mask, or a shell over another face.
No depicted target or contact establishes whether the tendrils cut, grasp, shield, or propel.

observations: Three materially different silhouettes are shown: a compact crouched humanoid encircled by many hooked tendrils, a very tall upright figure enclosed by four long vertical blades, and a stretched quadrupedal runner beneath one enormous overhead tail-blade.
The left candidate matches the master through the low crouch, oval head, long hands, and multiple radial hooked appendages.
The alternatives change height, locomotion, appendage count, and the relationship between body and blades rather than merely changing pose.
defects: In the selected left silhouette, several tendrils overlap the crouched limbs and one another, reducing clarity in the center.
The repeated hooked arcs create a circular emblem-like mass whose outline can be read before the humanoid stance.
uncertainties: The alternatives are not lettered or marked as selected; resemblance to the master is the only selection cue.

observations: The crop shows a featureless pale oval face angled toward the viewer with two glossy black eye cavities, a central vertical seam, and fine scratches but no brows, pupils, nose, cheeks, jaw articulation, or mouth.
The head tilt and direct orientation of both eye cavities create attention toward the viewer even though no conventional expression is available.
Seam lines continue down the neck into layered cord-like anatomy, visually joining the smooth head to the more fibrous torso.
defects: The absence of pupils and movable mouth or brow features prevents a precise immediate emotion from being distinguished through the face alone.
The two eye cavities are nearly symmetrical, so attention direction depends primarily on whole-head angle rather than eye focus.
uncertainties: The crop supports watchfulness or dissociation but does not unaided resolve desire, fear, or whether an intelligence is visible behind the cavities.

observations: At native size the pale oval head, crouched central body, long planted arms, and broad radial tendril halo remain recognizable.
The pale figure separates clearly from the dark appendages and background, preserving the body-versus-shadow contrast.
Eye cavities, body seams, symbols, finger claws, tendril serration, and most limb overlap collapse below reliable recognition.
defects: Individual tendrils merge into a dark circular mass, obscuring their count and functional direction.
The face reduces to a pale dot or mask shape, so its attention and absence of ordinary features are not independently readable.
uncertainties: The thumbnail preserves an alien stalker silhouette but not whether the surrounding arcs are weapons, limbs, wings, or effects.
